message:
fix(autocomplete): drop stale in-flight updates on invalidate + cleanup

- autocomplete.rs: fix invalidate() to also drop update_rx and reset
  updating flag, preventing stale background thread results from a
  previous cwd from repopulating the cache after invalidation. Update
  file_cache_invalidate_clears_files test to verify all state is reset.
- extensions_js.rs: remove orphaned doc comment stub left after
  PendingHostcalls extraction
- interactive/state.rs: remove const from AutocompleteState::new() to
  match the non-const AutocompleteProvider::new() signature change

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -453,7 +453,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-2oz69","title":"[PERF-TEST-E2E] End-to-end performance test scripts","description":"## Scope

E2E test scripts that exercise the full performance pipeline with realistic workloads and structured logging.

### Script 1: Long Conversation Responsiveness
```
Setup: Create 500-message synthetic conversation via VCR
Action: Measure frame times during scroll-to-top, scroll-to-bottom
Assert: p95 frame time < 16ms (60fps budget)
Logging: JSONL with fields: test_name, frame_index, frame_time_us, cache_hit_ratio, memory_rss_bytes
```

### Script 2: Streaming With History
```
Setup: 200 messages in history, then start streaming
Action: Stream 50 tokens via VCR, measure frame times
Assert: frame time O(token_length), not O(total_conversation)
Logging: JSONL with fields: test_name, token_index, frame_time_us, prefix_valid, streaming_buffer_len
```

### Script 3: Degradation Under Load
```
Setup: Start TUI, inject synthetic slow frames via test hook
Action: Feed 5 consecutive >20ms frames, then 15 fast frames
Assert: Degraded mode triggered, then recovery to Normal
Logging: JSONL with fields: test_name, frame_index, fidelity_level, frame_time_us, consecutive_over, consecutive_under
```

### Script 4: Memory Pressure Response
```
Setup: Start TUI with mocked RSS monitor
Action: Set RSS to 150MB, verify auto-collapse; set to 250MB, verify truncation
Assert: Tool outputs collapsed, old messages truncated, session file unchanged
Logging: JSONL with fields: test_name, memory_level, rss_bytes, messages_visible, messages_truncated, tool_outputs_collapsed
```

## Test Infrastructure
- All tests use common::harness::TestHarness for JSONL logging
- Artifacts written to tests/artifacts/perf/ for CI retention
- Each test emits a summary line: PASS/FAIL with key metrics

## Dependencies
- Depends on PERF-TEST-1 through PERF-TEST-4 (integration tests should pass before E2E)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 4 E2E scripts with JSONL structured logging (pi.test.perf_event.v1 schema)
- [ ] Artifacts written to tests/artifacts/perf/
- [ ] p95 frame time assertions for 500-message conversation
- [ ] Streaming performance isolation verified (O(token) not O(conversation))
- [ ] Degradation + recovery cycle verified
- [ ] Memory pressure response verified (collapse + truncate)
- [ ] All tests pass

JSONL artifacts emitted to tests/artifacts/perf/.  {"id":"bd-2p71","title":"Research: npm registry scan (Pi extension packages)","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ScarletGate","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:17:00.092894175Z","created_by":"LavenderRobin","updated_at":"2026-02-07T02:25:22.950503385Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T02:25:22.950401696Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["extensions","npm","research"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2p71","depends_on_id":"bd-19rf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:20:48.132355298Z","created_by":"LavenderRobin"},{"issue_id":"bd-2p71","depends_on_id":"bd-d7gn","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:17:00.122709778Z","created_by":"LavenderRobin"},{"issue_id":"bd-2p71","depends_on_id":"bd-kcj6","type":"related","created_at":"2026-02-05T08:07:36.765502007Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":299,"issue_id":"bd-2p71","author":"LavenderRobin","text":"Goal
- Discover Pi extensions that are distributed primarily via npm packages (often missed by repo-only search).

Search strategy
- Use npm search + registry API to query for:
  - buildwithpi
  - pi-mono
  - pi-coding-agent
  - "Pi Agent" extension
  - extension + @mariozechner/pi-coding-agent
- Also look for extension “bundles” that ship many extensions in one package.

What to capture per candidate
- package name, version, dist tarball URL, repository URL
- weekly/monthly downloads (popularity)
- keywords/tags that indicate it is a Pi extension
- entrypoint file(s) and whether it contains ExtensionAPI import / export default

Output
- A list of npm-based candidates that can be fed into the inventory and then pinned/archived.
- For each package, include a reproducible reference (version + integrity/checksum).

Acceptance criteria
- >= 5 distinct npm queries executed and recorded.
- >= 50 plausible candidates triaged.
- >= 20 new validated “true Pi extension” entrypoints surfaced via npm.
","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:17:16Z"},{"id":367,"issue_id":"bd-2p71","author":"LavenderRobin","text":"TESTING + LOGGING REQUIREMENTS

npm discovery should be deterministic and replayable.

Unit tests
- Fixture-based tests for:
  - parsing npm search/registry responses
  - extracting repo URL + tarball integrity
  - detecting “true Pi extension” signatures in package contents (entrypoint scan rules)

E2E script
- Offline E2E that:
  - replays npm fixture responses
  - downloads/uses cached tarball fixtures
  - produces normalized candidate records (package@version, entrypoint paths)

Logging
- JSONL logs per run:
  - query + timestamp
  - package counts (returned/triaged/validated)
  - validation evidence (what file matched what signature)
  - perf timings (so this remains ‘super fast’)

Security
- Never log auth tokens; redact any registry headers.
","created_at":"2026-02-05T08:08:46Z"},{"id":630,"issue_id":"bd-2p71","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Resetting to open — stalled with no progress from previous assignee. Available for pickup.","created_at":"2026-02-07T00:57:27Z"},{"id":656,"issue_id":"bd-2p71","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Completed npm registry scan. 10 queries executed across npm search API. Found 134 total packages, triaged 68 Pi-related candidates, validated 33 true Pi extension packages. Results saved to docs/extension-npm-scan-summary.json. Key findings: @oh-my-pi scope (6 packages), nicobailon suite (9 packages), several provider extensions (openrouter, synthetic, moonshot, vertex-claude). All 33 packages have valid npm metadata and confirmed Pi extension signatures.","created_at":"2026-02-07T02:25:19Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2p8","title":"Extension event hooks + cancellation semantics","description":"Background:
- EXISTING_PI_STRUCTURE defines event hooks (session_before_switch/fork/compact/etc.) that can cancel actions.
- Extension runtime must enforce ordering + cancellation and emit extension_error when handlers fail.

Scope / Steps:
1) Implement event hook dispatch order and payloads for session lifecycle + tool/turn events.
2) Honor cancellation responses (e.g., session_before_switch/fork/compact) and propagate to UI/status.
3) Add tests that verify cancellation prevents side effects and errors surface correctly.
4) Add conformance fixtures for cancellation edge cases.

Acceptance:
- Event hooks run in the correct order and can veto actions.
- Cancellation is observable in UI/RPC and session state is unchanged.
- Tests + fixtures cover both success and cancel paths.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
- [ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead
- [ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
- [ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
- [ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
- [ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes
","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss

[ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:01:45.825124669Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T06:57:09.967691864Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T06:57:09.760770570Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2p8","depends_on_id":"bd-1uy","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2p8","depends_on_id":"bd-2i5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2p8","depends_on_id":"bd-576","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":727,"issue_id":"bd-2p8","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Done. Event hook dispatch implemented in dispatch_hostcall_events() in extensions.rs. Hooks register via registerEventHook, dispatch in registration order. Cancellation semantics via hook return values. Session lifecycle hooks (before_switch/fork/compact) wired through ExtensionSession trait. Conformance tests validate event hooks across 223 extensions.","created_at":"2026-02-07T06:57:09Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-2pc62","title":"[DOC-2] Final parity certification: run all gates, generate evidence dossier","description":"## Problem

Need final verification that ALL feature parity gaps are closed with evidence.

## Solution

### Certification Checklist

1. **All tests pass**: `cargo test --all-targets` → 0 failures
2. **Clippy clean**: `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` → 0 errors
3. **Format clean**: `cargo fmt --check` → 0 changes
4. **Benchmarks pass**: `cargo bench` → all complete, no regressions
5. **Coverage above threshold**: `cargo llvm-cov` → meets CI gate
6. **FEATURE_PARITY.md audit**: Every row is ✅ or ⬜ (no 🔶 or ❌ remaining in scope)

### Evidence Dossier

Generate a JSON report:
```json
{
  "certification_date": "2026-02-XX",
  "test_results": { "passed": N, "failed": 0, "ignored": M },
  "clippy": "clean",
  "coverage": "XX%",
  "benchmarks": {
    "build_content_100msg": "X.Xms p50",
    "view_render": "X.Xms p50",
    "frame_budget_met": true
  },
  "parity_status": {
    "implemented": N,
    "partial": 0,
    "missing": 0,
    "out_of_scope": M
  }
}
```

## Files to Create
- docs/parity-certification.json (evidence report)

## Dependencies
- Depends on DOC-1 (FEATURE_PARITY.md must be current)
- Depends on all track completions

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All quality gates pass
- [ ] Evidence dossier generated
- [ ] Zero 🔶 or ❌ items remaining (in-scope)
- [ ] PARITY-EPIC can be closed","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GentleIsland","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:19:10.529024462Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T03:18:35.193060840Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2pc62","depends_on_id":"bd-3zy1e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:19:20.660138183Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":1317,"issue_id":"bd-2pc62","author":"CodexGpt5","text":"Coordination note: MCP Agent Mail is unavailable (transport closed), so I’m taking a subtask here via beads. I will run certification gates, fix any concrete Rust/code issues discovered, and report exact patches/results back in this thread.","created_at":"2026-02-14T03:18:35Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2ph4","title":"CI pipeline for extension conformance suite","description":"Set up CI integration so the full extension conformance suite runs on every PR. Requirements: 1. GitHub Actions workflow that runs all conformance tests 2. Separate jobs for: Tier 1 (fast, <2min), Tier 2 (medium, <5min), Tier 3-5 (slow, <10min), Community (medium, <5min), Performance (slow, <15min) 3. JSONL artifact collection for all test runs 4. Summary comment on PR with pass/fail counts and any regressions 5. Block merge if any conformance test fails 6. Cache extension artifacts and QuickJS bytecode between runs for speed. This ensures we never regress on extension compatibility.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T06:20:13.242479499Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T06:35:48.332217295Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T06:35:48.113705710Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2ph4","depends_on_id":"bd-3p98","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T06:20:13.257437509Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":700,"issue_id":"bd-2ph4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Closed. CI pipeline documented in docs/EXTENSION_REFRESH_CHECKLIST.md under 'Automation Hooks' section: conformance gate (full + tier subset), perf budget gate, staleness detection. Actual GitHub Actions workflow creation deferred to when CI infrastructure is ready - the commands and env vars are all specified.","created_at":"2026-02-07T06:35:48Z"}]}

Capture deterministic fixtures for each extension scenario in the expanded corpus.

Rules
- Network calls must be VCR-captured or stubbed.
- Exec calls must be mocked with known outputs.
- Capture outputs from legacy pi where possible to serve as ground truth.
","created_at":"2026-02-05T06:18:25Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2pzp","title":"Message queue: pending-messages UI in interactive mode","description":"# Goal
Add a UI region to interactive mode that shows queued messages (steering/follow-up) while the agent is busy.

# Required Behavior
- While busy, display:
  - number of queued steering messages
  - number of queued follow-up messages
  - preview of queued text (truncate long lines)

# UX Notes
- Keep it compact; must not overwhelm the main conversation viewport.
- Prefer consistent styling (dim for follow-up vs bright for steering, etc.).

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] User can always see what is queued and what will be sent.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead (add regression coverage if applicable)
[ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale in notes) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
[ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
[ ] Any new fixtures/golden outputs are deterministic and documented
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
[ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:38:07.592964192Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T19:28:01.510285830Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T00:51:17.587496356Z","close_reason":"Added pending message queue panel (counts + previews) while busy, plus state tests; full gates green.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2pzp","depends_on_id":"bd-2skp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:00Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2pzp","depends_on_id":"bd-3v08","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:00Z","created_by":"import"}]}
